questions.question: What problem does the Q-Former solve between a frozen ViT and a frozen LLM?
options: Feeding all 256 patch tokens into the LLM wastes context (8,192 tokens for a batch of 32 images) — the Q-Former compresses each image to 32 visual tokens, trained without touching either frozen backbone
questions.explanation: 32 learnable queries cross-attend over patch tokens to produce a compact visual summary. 188M trained parameters bridge an 11B-class stack — the template for every adapter-based VLM through 2026.

questions.question: What are "learnable queries" in the Q-Former?
options: User questions cached from production traffic
32 parameter vectors — the same for every image — that attend to patch tokens and each end up holding a compressed slice of the image, learning whatever encoding makes the losses drop
SQL queries generated by the LLM
questions.explanation: The inversion is the trick: instead of text tokens attending to patches, dedicated query vectors do the looking. They don't literally specialize on nameable concepts; they learn whatever summary serves the objectives.

questions.question: What are BLIP-2's two training stages?
options: Pretrain on text, finetune on images
Stage 1: representation learning with ITC + ITM + ITG losses (no LLM, frozen ViT, only Q-Former trains); Stage 2: attach the frozen LLM, train only the Q-Former + a linear projection on LM loss
questions.explanation: Contrastive, matching, and image-grounded-generation losses first teach the queries to encode text-generatable content; only then does the bridge meet the LLM. Inference: image → ViT → Q-Former → projection → prepended to text.

questions.question: What did BLIP-2's parameter economics demonstrate?
options: Training just 188M bridge parameters (~2.4% of the 8B stack) matched or beat Flamingo-80B on zero-shot VQA — days on a few A100s instead of weeks end-to-end
questions.explanation: The trainable-bottleneck-between-frozen-giants pattern won on both cost and stability, and seeded MiniGPT-4, InstructBLIP, and the LLaVA family.

questions.question: What did InstructBLIP add to the Q-Former?
options: The instruction text as an extra input at cross-attention time, so queries specialize per-instruction ("count the cars" vs "describe the mood") instead of producing one fixed summary
questions.explanation: Instruction-aware querying yielded benchmark gains on held-out tasks — the visual summary becomes conditioned on what the user actually asked.
